leads.title: Withings HFRN Retrospective Study
leads.summary: <cite index="2-1">Withings develops connected health devices</cite> for remote patient monitoring and clinical research, with a strong track record in retrospective studies to validate their wearable technology.
leads.pi: Pierre Escourrou, MD, PhD (Centre Interdisciplinaire du Sommeil, Paris, France)
leads.channel: Direct to VP Business Development via LinkedIn; reference their Heart Failure/HFRN research trajectory and clinical ambitions in Eastern Europe.
leads.who: <cite index="13-1">Jean-Baptiste Fontes, VP Business Development and Strategic Partnerships</cite>
leads.angle: Your HFRN retrospective study on heart failure is expanding—Mediogram adds high-volume Eastern European sites, regulatory expertise (including cardiac), and local ethics/imaging QC to accelerate enrollment and strengthen data quality.
leads.opener: Jean-Baptiste, Mediogram runs high-enrolling cardiovascular sites across Eastern Europe (Minsk, Belarus—RNPC Cardiology) with built-in regulatory, ethics, imaging QC, and logistics. We saw your HFRN retrospective launching from France and think your HF cohort could benefit from our regional capacity and quality infrastructure. Would be worth a 20-minute call to explore fit. — Mediogram BD

leads.title: A Multi-site, Prospective, Non-interventional, Observational Data Collection Study in Patients Undergoing a Right Heart Catheterization
leads.summary: <cite index="5-3,5-4">Acorai is a Swedish medtech startup developing non-invasive devices that use machine learning to monitor cardiac and pulmonary pressure, aiming to reduce heart failure hospitalizations</cite>, backed by <cite index="1-3">Solardis Health Ventures, KHP Ventures, Carma Fund and other US investors</cite>, with <cite index="7-4,6-2,6-3">a recent $4.5M investment from the EIC Fund, Taiwan Medical University, and private investors in late 2024</cite>.
leads.channel: Email directly to Rita Peixoto (study contact) with context-setting CC to Kasper Bourdette (COO & Head of Commercialization)
leads.who: <cite index="22-2">Kasper Bourdette, Co-founder & Head of Commercialization</cite>
leads.angle: <cite index="7-6">The CAPTURE-HF observational study has completed enrollment at 20 hospitals across 6 countries with 1,600+ patients</cite>; Mediogram can accelerate site activation and outcomes data capture in Eastern Europe for their next phase expansion.
leads.opener: Hi Rita – we follow Acorai's impressive CAPTURE-HF progress and your planned non-interventional RHC observational work. Mediogram (RNPC Cardiology, Minsk) specializes in high-enrolling cardiology sites + regulatory/ethics/imaging QC across Eastern Europe. We'd welcome a brief conversation with Kasper on adding compliant, efficient sites to your observational pipeline. – Mediogram Business Development

leads.title: Pivotal Trial to Evaluate the Safety and Effectiveness of the P&F TricValve® Transcatheter Bicaval Valve System
leads.summary: P&F Products & Features is a Vienna-based medtech innovator with FDA Breakthrough Device status for TricValve, a minimally invasive bicaval valve system for severe tricuspid regurgitation—they need high-enrolling sites and proven regulatory/imaging QC expertise in their pivotal U.S. trial.
leads.channel: Direct email to study contact Thuy Olender (tolender@productsandfeatures.com / +1 408-839-3809), with cc or soft intro to clinical operations leadership.
leads.who: <cite index="12-3,12-15">Dr. Katharina Kiss (CEO, Co-Founder) and Dr. Siegfried Einhellig (President and COO)</cite>; escalate via study contact for Clinical Operations lead.
leads.angle: Mediogram's Eastern Europe cardiovascular CRO model—owned regulatory/ethics approvals, imaging QC, and high-enrolling sites—directly accelerates TRICAV II pivotal enrollment and reduces site initiation timelines.
leads.opener: Hi Thuy—Mediogram runs cardiology device trials across Eastern Europe (Minsk, high-enrolling sites + owned imaging QC and regulatory/ethics setup). Given TRICAV II's pivotal scope and timeline, we'd like to discuss how our Eastern European footprint and integrated clinical-execution model can support enrollment acceleration. Would Dr. Kiss or the clinical ops lead have 15 minutes to explore? Best, Mediogram BD Team

leads.title: Single-arm, Multi-center Study Evaluating the Acceptance and Feasibility of a Digital Remote Monitoring Tool Configured for Patients With ATTR-CM: ACO-Monitor
leads.summary: Bayer is a global pharmaceutical and life sciences company headquartered in Germany with multiple cardiovascular and advanced therapy programs; they run decentralized early-phase trials with emphasis on digital innovation and patient-centric operations.
leads.channel: Christoph Koenen (Head of Global Clinical Development & Operations, Pharma R&D) via clinical-trials-contact@bayer.com; reference Oliver Renner (Head of Business Development & Licensing) for deal partnership.
leads.who: Dr. Christoph Koenen, Head of Global Clinical Development and Operations, Pharmaceuticals R&D; alternatively Oliver Renner, Head of Business Development & Licensing, Pharmaceuticals.
leads.angle: ACO-Monitor's digital remote monitoring model aligns directly with Bayer's stated strategy of operationalizing patient-first R&D—80%+ of their clinical studies shaped by patient input—and leveraging digital health tools as backbone of clinical innovation, especially in cardiovascular where Koenen has deep expertise.
leads.opener: We've followed Bayer's pivot toward patient-driven, digitally-enabled clinical trials (May 2026 thought leadership). Mediogram offers a high-efficiency alternative for your ATTR-CM European sites: we add 3 proven cardiology centers in Eastern Europe with full regulatory/ethics/imaging QC and logistics owned locally, reducing site activation timelines while capturing the remote monitoring data Bayer values. Shall we explore a collaboration model for ACO-Monitor's next expansion?

leads.title: Vsling™ III: Clinical Evaluation of the Vsling™ Device for Ventricular Repair in Patients with Heart Failure
conditions: HFrEF - Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction
leads.summary: Cardiac Success is an Israel-based medtech company developing the Vsling™, a transcatheter ventricular repair device for HFrEF patients, backed by 415 CAPITAL and strategic structural heart investors; they are in early clinical stages seeking to expand trial sites.
leads.channel: Direct outreach via Iris Yadin-Herling (existing study contact) + LinkedIn outreach to Boaz Manash (CEO) on clinical operations/site development strategy
leads.who: Boaz Manash, CEO (no titled head of Clinical Operations identified publicly; work through Iris Yadin-Herling or approach CEO directly)
leads.angle: Mediogram's proven Eastern Europe cardiovascular trial delivery (Minsk-based RNPC Cardiology, FIH/early-feasibility expertise, regulatory + ethics + imaging QC) can accelerate Vsling III enrollment and ensure quality data from high-enrolling HFrEF sites before broader European rollout.
leads.opener: Hi Iris & Boaz—Mediogram has successfully executed early-phase cardiovascular device trials in Eastern Europe (RNPC Cardiology, Minsk), with capabilities in regulatory navigation, ethics approvals, imaging QC, and rapid site activation. For Vsling III, we can add HFrEF-experienced sites and manage operational complexity so your team stays focused on clinical outcomes. Would you be open to a brief call to discuss how we might support Phase 2 enrollment? Best, Mediogram BD

leads.title: Globe Registry: Globe® Pulsed Field System Registry Study for Treating Patients With Symptomatic Atrial Fibrillation
leads.summary: Kardium is a Vancouver-based medical device company developing the Globe Pulsed Field System for atrial fibrillation treatment, recently backed by $250M in Series 8 funding and FDA-approved in 2025.
leads.channel: Clinical Affairs contact (Clinical.Affairs@kardium.com / +1 604-248-8891) who can route to Stefan Avall or commercial leadership.
leads.who: Stefan Avall, Vice President, Product Management (oversees commercial launch, clinical research, and PULSAR trial execution).
leads.angle: Position Mediogram's Eastern European capacity & regulatory expertise to accelerate Globe Registry enrollment and site activation in post-CE mark markets where Kardium is expanding clinical data beyond paroxysmal AF.
leads.opener: Hi Clinical Affairs team – we're Mediogram, a clinical-execution partner with high-enrolling cardiology sites and local regulatory/ethics expertise across Eastern Europe (Minsk, etc.). We see Globe Registry ramping post-FDA approval, and we'd like to discuss how our platform could support accelerated registry enrollment and site ops in your European/post-CE markets. Would Stefan Avall's team be open to a brief call?

leads.title: Catheter Ablation Using a Novel Navigation and Imaging System
leads.summary: <cite index="2-5">LUMA Vision develops an innovative 4D imaging and navigation end-to-end platform for cardiac applications</cite> seeking high-enrolling, early-feasibility trial sites in Europe.
leads.channel: Direct to Elke Sommerijns via email, copy CEO Fionn Lahart—they are actively running first-in-human work (LUMINIZE trial announced Jan 2025) and fundraising momentum indicates clinical expansion priority.
leads.who: Elke Sommerijns (Study Contact/Clinical Operations) or Fionn Lahart, CEO (for partnership-level discussion); <cite index="1-17">company is led by investors Life Sciences Partners and imec.xpand</cite>, signaling clinical-execution partnerships align with their growth strategy.
leads.angle: <cite index="18-13,18-14">LUMA Vision recently received FDA clearance (April 2025) for VERAFEYE platform</cite>, creating urgent European clinical validation need—catheter ablation for atrial fibrillation fits their core indication strategy and fast-track economics.
leads.opener:
Hi Elke & Fionn,

Mediaogram runs high-enrolling cardiovascular device trials across Eastern Europe (Minsk-based RNPC Cardiology with regulatory/ethics/imaging QC infrastructure). Your catheter ablation AFib trial aligns with our FIH/early-feasibility focus—we've placed similar early-stage sites and expedite patient flow. Given VERAFEYE's FDA clearance momentum, we'd discuss adding 1–2 high-volume sites in Belarus/region for accelerated enrollment.

Would you have 20 min next week?

Mediaogram BD

Hummingbird ICE Study
conditions: ATRIAL APPENDAGE CLOSURE for ATRIAL FIBRILLATION
leads.summary: <cite index="6-2,6-3">Philips is a diversified technology company headquartered in Amsterdam with €21.3B market cap and 10,000+ employees</cite>, and <cite index="4-1,4-11">received a recent March 2025 post-IPO investment from Exor</cite> that aligns with structural-heart clinical programs.
leads.channel: Direct to Luciano Mazzaro (current study contact) with executive escalation to <cite index="17-2,17-4">Christina Villar, Head of Global Clinical Operations</cite> or <cite index="12-1">Dr. Sanjay Gandhi, VP Head of Medical Affairs</cite>
leads.who: <cite index="17-2,17-4">Christina Villar, Head of Global Clinical Operations Philips</cite>
leads.angle: <cite index="21-1">Philips' VeriSight Pro 3D ICE catheter is advancing LAA occlusion procedures</cite>—Mediogram can execute the Hummingbird ICE trial in Eastern Europe with high-enrolling cardiovascular centers, regulatory expertise, and imaging QC for this early-feasibility device study.
leads.opener: Luciano—we help sponsors like Philips de-risk early-stage structural-heart trials by adding proven enrollment and imaging-QC infrastructure in regulated markets. Mediogram runs FIH/EFS studies across Eastern Europe (Minsk, Budapest, Prague). For your Hummingbird ICE program, we can open 4–6 high-volume LAAO sites with ethics pre-approval and local regulatory pathway mapped. Let's align on timeline and protocol—Mediogram

leads.title: Parasternal Extravascular ICD System Pivotal Clinical Investigation (ALARION EV) Study
leads.summary: AtaCor Medical is a VC-backed (Series D, $149M raised) extravascular ICD innovator launching their flagship ALARION EV pivotal trial this year, backed by top-tier cardiac device investors.
leads.channel: Direct outreach via Sarah Hase (study contact) with copy to Alan Marcovecchio (VP Clinical, Regulatory & Quality) emphasizing site capabilities
leads.who: Alan Marcovecchio, VP Clinical, Regulatory & Quality (or Director of Clinical Operations)
leads.angle: Your ALARION EV pivotal requires diverse European sites with robust cardiac imaging QC and ethics speed—Mediogram owns that in Eastern Europe.
leads.opener: Hi Alan/Sarah – We saw AtaCor is launching ALARION EV across US and Europe in 2026. Mediogram runs high-enrolling cardiovascular device trials in Eastern Europe (RNPC Cardiology in Minsk, FIH specialists, in-house imaging QC, ethics coordination). We'd like to discuss whether substernal ICD sites in our region fit your enrollment strategy. Happy to send site capability overview. Best, Mediogram BD

leads.title: Cryoablation for Monomorphic Ventricular Tachycardia (VTS)
leads.summary: <cite index="3-2,3-4">Adagio Medical is a medical device company developing proprietary Ultra-Low Temperature Cryoablation technology for treatment of cardiac arrhythmias, currently focused on ventricular tachycardia with their CE-marked vCLAS™ catheter.</cite>
leads.channel: Email to Deborah Kaster (Chief Business Officer) referencing Nabil Jubran; also direct to njubran@adagiomedical.com with trial governance framing.
leads.who: <cite index="16-1,16-3">Deborah Kaster, Chief Financial Officer and Chief Business Officer (oversees business development and corporate strategy)</cite>
leads.angle: <cite index="2-3">Adagio closed $19M funding in October 2025</cite> and is advancing FULCRUM-VT pivotal trial; Mediogram's Eastern European cardiac trial infrastructure can accelerate enrollment and support their commercial-stage transition.
leads.opener: Nabil and team: Congratulations on your recent funding close and positive FULCRUM-VT data momentum. Mediogram (cardiac trials in Eastern Europe—RNPC Cardiology Minsk, regulatory/QC/logistics in-house) could be a strategic partner to expand your monomorphic-VT cryoablation trial footprint and de-risk enrollment in high-enrolling geographies. We'd welcome a conversation with Deborah on how Mediogram fits your US pivotal and post-market strategy. —Mediogram BD

leads.title: VT Cryoablation System Post-Market Clinical Follow-up (PMCF) Study
leads.summary: <cite index="3-2">Adagio Medical is a medical device company focused on developing and commercializing products for the treatment of cardiac arrhythmias utilizing its novel, proprietary, catheter-based Ultra-Low Temperature Cryoablation (UTLC) technology</cite>, and they fit because their VT cryoablation system is CE Marked and commercializing across Europe.
leads.channel: Direct outreach to Deborah Kaster (Chief Business Officer) via email or LinkedIn, referencing the trial contact Nabil Jubran
leads.who: <cite index="15-5,15-7">Deborah Kaster, Chief Business Officer (and Chief Financial Officer), who oversees business development, corporate strategy and investor relations</cite>
leads.angle: <cite index="2-3">Adagio recently closed a $19M funding round in October 2025</cite>, positioning them to accelerate UK PMCF enrollment to build real-world evidence for their vCLAS system ahead of anticipated FDA approval by end-2026.
leads.opener: Hi Deborah – Mediogram runs high-enrolling cardiovascular device trials across Eastern Europe (Minsk cardiology center, FIH specialist, imaging QC, regulatory). We've identified Adagio's VT Cryoablation PMCF study launching in the UK and believe we can accelerate site activation and enrollment in parallel European markets. Given your recent funding and FDA timeline, let's discuss how Mediogram can support your clinical execution strategy. Best, Mediogram BD

leads.title: Sympathetic Ablation for Ventricular Arrhythmias
leads.summary: SymKardia is a cardiac neuromodulation device company developing sympathetic ablation therapy for treatment-resistant ventricular arrhythmias, addressing a high-mortality indication with unmet clinical need.
leads.channel: Direct outreach to Imad Libbus (study contact listed) via email + LinkedIn; follow with CEO Bruce KenKnight's office
leads.who: Bruce KenKnight, CEO (primary) / Imad Libbus, PhD (immediate study contact, likely VP Clinical Development or Chief Medical Officer)
leads.angle: Eastern European early-feasibility sites in cardiac electrophysiology—Mediogram can accelerate FIH enrollment with established RNPC Cardiology infrastructure, regulatory expertise, and imaging QC for sympathetic denervation outcomes tracking in Minsk region.
leads.opener: Hi Imad—We noticed your sympathetic ablation VT trial entering recruitment phase. Mediogram specializes in FIH/early-feasibility support in Eastern Europe with proven high-enrolling sites and local regulatory expertise in cardiac electrophysiology. We'd welcome a brief call to discuss how adding RNPC Cardiology (Minsk) and our regional network could accelerate patient access and trial timelines. Best—[Your Name], BD Lead, Mediogram

leads.summary: MicroPort EP is a Shanghai-based, well-funded medical device company (backed by CITIC PE, E Fund, and others) developing electrophysiology solutions including high-density mapping catheters for arrhythmia treatment.
leads.channel: Direct outreach via LinkedIn to MicroPort EP's Clinical Operations/Medical Affairs lead; secondary: Dr. Yiyong Sun's office or investor relations given recent funding rounds
leads.who: Head of Clinical Operations or Clinical Affairs (contact via company website/LinkedIn—specific name not publicly listed; escalate to Dr. Yiyong Sun, President if needed)
leads.angle: EasyStars™ trial launching now offers perfect timing to demonstrate clinical validation in high-enrolling, FIH-experienced Eastern European sites with proven arrhythmia case volumes and local regulatory expertise.
leads.opener: We've noted your EasyStars™ efficiency trial is preparing to launch. MicroPort EP's technology aligns perfectly with our RNPC Cardiology footprint in Eastern Europe—we bring proven rapid enrollment in VT/AF cases, direct relationships with top cardiac centers in Belarus/region, and established imaging QC/regulatory pathways. Let's explore how we can accelerate your trial timelines. — Mediogram

leads.summary: MicroPort EP is China's leading integrated electrophysiology device company with NMPA-approved products, magnetic navigation tech, and recent private equity backing from CITIC/E Fund—they're expanding clinical evidence globally.
leads.pi: Gao Henggan (Ruijin-Hainan Hospital Shanghai Jiao Tong University School Of Medicine)
leads.channel: Email contact: Qi NA Jin, PhD (study contact listed) via ruijin@rjh.com.cn with CC to MicroPort EP's Clinical Affairs lead (identify via LinkedIn/company website before outreach).
leads.who: Clinical Operations/Clinical Affairs Director or Medical Affairs VP—escalate through Qi if needed to Dr. Yiyong Sun (President, as mentioned in recent funding announcements).
leads.angle: Your magnetic navigation ablation catheter trial in supraventricular tachycardia targets the exact arrhythmia gap MicroPort is addressing—Eastern Europe expansion validates your NMPA/CE-cleared platform with high-enrolling sites and imaging QC.
leads.opener: Dr. Qi, We're Mediogram (RNPC's BD partner in Eastern Europe). We've designed a network of high-enrolling cardiovascular sites with local regulatory/imaging QC that accelerates feasibility and pivotal trials for devices like your magnetic navigation RF catheter. We'd like to discuss how we can support your rapid-arrhythmia trial's enrollment and QC in CIS markets. Can we set up a brief call with your clinical operations team?
